title: String Pool Optimization
content: String literals in Java are stored in a special memory region called the 'String Constant Pool' (located inside the heap). This allows JVM to reuse matching literals and conserve memory.

title: Garbage Collection Hint
content: Running System.gc() does not guarantee JVM will run GC immediately. It is merely a suggestion to the JVM that garbage collection may be helpful.

title: Double vs BigDecimal
content: Never use float or double for precise values like currency or financial calculations. Always use BigDecimal to avoid rounding errors caused by binary representation of decimals.

title: Java 8 Optional
content: Optional should be used primarily as a return type for methods that may not return a value. Avoid using Optional as field types or method arguments as it is not serializable.

title: Immutable Collections
content: Java 9 introduced List.of(), Set.of(), and Map.of() factory methods. These return unmodifiable collections which are highly memory efficient and thread-safe.
